office: Select Committee on the Modernization of Congress
description: The Select Committee on the Modernization of Congress is looking for a summer research intern beginning in July 2020. The Select Committee's final report will be submitted to the House in October 2020. The intern will assist in drafting and proofreading the report as well as archiving documents and committee activity in preparation for termination of the Committee at the end of the 116th Congress. We are looking for a flexible individual that would be able to work both in person and remotely as needed. The internship may last up to 120 days (4 months). About Select Committee on the Modernization of Congress: We are a unique Select Committee. We work as a nonpartisan staff for a bipartisan committee of 12 Members. The Select Committee on the Modernization of Congress has been tasked to investigate, study, make findings, hold public hearings, and develop recommendations on modernizing Congress.
responsibilities: Utilize House resources to research and write memoranda on Congressional history
Assist in drafting and proofreading the Select Committee's final report
Archive documents and committee activity in preparation for termination of the Committee
qualifications: Strong reading, writing, and speaking skills
Ability to work in a nonpartisan manner
Self-starter able to self-motivate during slow periods
Experience in research and archiving is a plus
how_to_apply: Interested candidates should send a cover letter and their resume to Michael.Massiwer@mail.house.gov

description:
The Republican office of the Committee on House Administration seeks energetic and self-motivated individuals with an interest in government and public policy, to apply for its full-time Fall 2020 internship program.

Working closely with Committee staff, interns will have the opportunity to gain first-hand knowledge of the inner-workings of a congressional committee and learn about the day-to-day administrative operations of the House of Representatives.

Interns are responsible for assisting Committee staff with official proceedings (hearings/markups), oversight, research, assisting with phone inquiries, and providing support to all Committee staff.

*Due to the ongoing Covid-19 pandemic, this internship opportunity is subject to change.
responsibilities: Assist Committee staff with official proceedings (hearings/markups)
Provide support to all Committee staff
qualifications: Interest in government and public policy
how_to_apply: To apply, please visit https://republicans-cha.house.gov/ and submit application by July 20th 2020.

description:
Congresswoman Elaine G. Luria (VA-02) member of the House Armed Services Committee and House Veterans Affairs Committee, where the Congresswoman serves as Chair of the Disability Assistance and Memorial Affairs Subcommittee, seeks a paid press intern for her Washington DC office.

Responsibilities may include drafting social media content, monitoring social media platforms, drafting constituent correspondence and outreach, and assisting the Communications Team with other tasks as needed. The press intern will report to and work closely with the Press Assistant and the Communications Director.

Qualified candidates should be motivated, detail-oriented, a team player, and possess strong oral and written communication skills. Virginia ties are a plus. Women, LGBTQ+, and minority candidates are encouraged to apply. All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age.
responsibilities: Assist the Communications Team with other tasks as needed
qualifications: Possess strong oral and written communication skills
how_to_apply: To apply, please email a single PDF including your cover letter listing your availability, resume and a 1-2 page writing sample to VA02DC.Interns@mail.house.gov with "Your Name: VA-02 Fall Press Internship" in the subject line. Please no drop-ins or phone calls.
equal_opportunity: Women, LGBTQ+, and minority candidates are encouraged to apply. All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age.

description: Congresswoman Elaine G. Luria (VA-02) member of the House Armed Services Committee and House Veterans Affairs Committee, where the Congresswoman serves as Chair of the Disability Assistance and Memorial Affairs Subcommittee, seeks paid interns for her Washington, D.C. office.
qualifications: Strong oral and written communication skills
Virginia ties are a plus but not required
how_to_apply: To apply, please email a single PDF including your cover letter listing your availability, resume and 1-2 page writing sample to VA02DC.Interns@mail.house.gov with "Your Name: VA-02 Fall Internship" in the subject line. Please no drop-ins or phone calls.
equal_opportunity: All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age. Women, LGBTQ+, and minority candidates are encouraged to apply.

description:
The Office of Congressman Harris is currently seeking candidates for their Fall 2020 Internship Program in their three District offices, located throughout the Eastern Shore of Maryland.

A Congressional Internship is a valuable way to understand Congressional office operations, the legislative process, and the importance of serving others.
responsibilities: Providing excellent service to constituents in-person and over-the-phone including greeting constituents and answering telephones
Assisting with special projects, including legislative research
Involvement with additional special projects important to the Congressman's office
qualifications: Familiarity with Microsoft Office including Outlook and Excel
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should visit harris.house.gov to read additional details about the internship application and email a completed application to jonathan.glaser@mail.house.gov

description:
Congressman Ron Kind's Washington, DC office seeks a paid legislative fellow to work on his Ways and Means Health Subcommittee portfolio for the rest of 2020.

The fellow will assist the Member and staff with all aspects of health care policy, with a particular focus on Medicare policy. The fellow will assist with analyzing and developing legislation, drafting memos and letters, and attending constituent meetings.

Candidates should have strong writing and organizational skills, operate well in a fast paced environment, and work well as part of a team. Applicants must be detail oriented, maintain strict confidentiality and exceptional communication skills. Experience in health policy or a graduate degree in a relevant field preferred.
responsibilities: Work on Ways and Means Health Subcommittee portfolio
Assist the Member and staff with all aspects of health care policy
qualifications: Operate well in a fast paced environment
Work well as part of a team
Experience in health policy or a graduate degree in a relevant field preferred
how_to_apply: To apply for a fellowship, please send a cover letter and resume to carter.macleod@mail.house.gov

position_title: Labor Policy Intern or Labor Policy Fellow
office: Committee on Education and Labor, Majority
description:
The Committee on Education and Labor, Majority is currently seeking a second or third year graduate or law student as a 'Labor Policy Intern' or 'Labor Policy Fellow' for the spring of 2020. Interns and Fellows perform a variety of tasks that support the staff in our legislative work.

Legislative tasks may include, but are not limited to: legislative research, legal research, and fact checking; technical writing including legislation, memos, and speeches; assisting staff in hearing and markup preparation and execution; and attending other hearings, markups, briefings and meetings for the purpose of informing staff and Members as they work to craft legislation and policy proposals.

Applicants should be able to start immediately and commit through May. Graduates and Fellows should be able to demonstrate study, practical or policy experience in issues relevant to the Committee. Fellows must be participating in an established fellowship program.
responsibilities: Conduct legislative research, legal research, and fact checking
Draft technical writing including legislation, memos, and speeches
Assist staff in hearing and markup preparation and execution
Attend hearings, markups, briefings, and meetings to inform staff and Members as they work to craft legislation and policy proposals
qualifications: Second or third year graduate or law student
Ability to start immediately and commit through May
Demonstrate study, practical, or policy experience in issues relevant to the Committee
Fellows must be participating in an established fellowship program
how_to_apply: Email cover letter, resume, and internship applicant information form to E&L_Interns&Fellows@mail.house.gov. Include two short, relevant writing samples in application materials. Applicants may visit https://edlabor.house.gov/about/internships for more information.
